narrative: OMX30 opens in a precarious state Tuesday morning. Global equities traded lower overnight (S&P -0.79%, NASDAQ -1.55%, DAX/CAC -0.43%/-0.63%), creating a -0.301% correlation headwind, while US 10Y yields surged +0.88% to 4.609%. The index sits +5.7% above its 200-MA but only +0.2% above the 50-MA, indicating near-term consolidation pressure. Technicals are conflicted: RSI 49 is neutral, MACD has turned below signal (hist=-7.245), and critically, ADX is extremely weak at 4.6 with bearish directional bias—this mandates a 30% confidence reduction per protocol. The Stochastic K/D (7.2/25.2) flags oversold conditions, creating mean-reversion risk. Commodity strength (copper +2.37%, Brent +2.93%, gold +0.90%) offers sector-specific lifts but also hints at demand concerns and cost inflation.
morningBrief: OMX30 expected to trade sideways to slightly lower on weak global momentum and conflicting signals. The index is 3.3% below its 52-week high and faces headwinds: rising yields compress tech/real estate valuations, while oil strength pressures industrials' input costs. Key supports at 3085 (lower Bollinger band) and 3123 (yesterday close); resistance at 3180 (50-MA). ADX at 4.6 means trend conviction is near zero—respect technical levels tightly. Commodity rally supports Boliden (copper narrative), but watch for industrial margin pressure in Atlas Copco/Volvo B. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) catch a modest NIM bid from higher yields but don't chase—Stochastic oversold signals potential bounce, not breakout. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is today's macro catalyst; any surprise could reignite risk-off. Positioning: reduce leverage, tighten stops, avoid breakout chasing.
drivers: Weighted correlation -0.301% signals mild risk-off despite Asia strength
US yields +0.88% creating tech/real estate headwinds; banks supported but magnitude dampened by weak ADX
Commodity strength (Brent +2.93%, Copper +2.37%) mixed: positive for Boliden, negative for industrial input costs
OMX30 technicals conflicted: bull regime vs RSI neutral, MACD below signal, Stochastic oversold, ADX critically weak at 4.6

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +0.88% to 4.609% widens NIM expectations; however, weak ADX and conflicted technicals limit upside conviction.
NASDAQ -1.55% overnight + rising yields compress DCF multiples; Ericsson sentiment headwind from risk-off.
US 10Y +0.88% raises refinancing costs and caps valuations; likely sector weakness today.
Copper +2.37%, gold +0.90%—direct revenue tailwind for Boliden; SSAB benefits from mixed commodity backdrop.
Brent +2.93% raises input cost inflation fears; offsetting benefit from USD/SEK +0.15% (exporter tailwind) only partial.
Oil strength pressures consumer spending sentiment; EUR/SEK +0.08% marginally worsens H&M sourcing costs.

watchList.reason: Copper +2.37% and gold +0.90% are direct revenue drivers; strong commodity backdrop supports mining sector.
USD/SEK +0.15% is positive for exporter, but Brent +2.93% adds cost headwind; net neutral—monitor earnings outlook.
watchList.catalyst: FX strength vs oil input costs

watchList.reason: NASDAQ -1.55% overnight and risk-off sentiment weigh on tech; avoid until broader tech stabilizes.
Yields +0.88% support NIM; however, weak ADX means limit position size until trend clarifies.
Oil and FX headwinds; avoid until consumer sentiment signal improves.

keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (yesterday close—hold here for neutral, break below triggers sell signal)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is key macro catalyst—expect volatility if print surprises materially.
topRisk: Escalation in geopolitical/trade tensions or surprise weak US employment data could spike VIX (now 17.21) and trigger OMX30 break below 3085, accelerating selloff. Rising yield environment also risks further real estate/tech capitulation.
technicalSummary: But: MACD below signal (hist=-7.245), ADX critically weak at 4.6, Stochastic oversold K=7.2
RSI 49 is neutral—no overbought/oversold mean reversion flag
%B=0.19 shows price in lower half of Bollinger bands—room to upside but weak momentum
52-week: 86% of range, -3.3% from highs—near extremes, not extended
